steps.title: Find the key paradox
steps.content: The passage reveals a paradox at the heart of the study: users experienced disappointment but then employed cognitive mechanisms to reverse those feelings, showing that negative disconfirmation can actually lead to positive outcomes.

steps.title: Match to the main idea
steps.content: C correctly identifies the finding: users with high expectations can feel content despite negative disconfirmation. The other three either contradict this, introduce irrelevant points, or misrepresent the research. That is the correct answer.
